rag_rules: For named-thing type errors, trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper on the reported line; fix that root cause or drop the invalid clause.
If a clause uses names outside the current scope, keep only scope-visible references or replace the clause with a valid local binding; do not expand architecture.
generic_rules: Make the smallest text edit that removes the validator error and preserves unrelated content.
Prefer deleting or rewriting an invalid contract clause over inventing new model elements.
Treat repeated diagnostics as possibly sharing one root cause before making multiple edits.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Only visible features and locally declared helpers with valid types are safe references in a clause.
assign needs a typed left-hand side that already exists as an output or auxiliary; comparison results are bool, integer expressions are int, and real expressions are real.
anti_patterns: Do not leave unresolved names, scope-invalid paths, or type-mismatched helpers in the repaired text.
Do not move or add unrelated architecture, connections, or properties just to silence a clause-level error.
